lora.name: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)
lora.base_freedom_notes: Standard LoRA learns rank-r deltas on top of frozen weights. Single LoRA cannot encode full-rank base knowledge. However, ReLoRA (Lialin et al. 2023) shows that iteratively merging low-rank updates achieves full-rank training from scratch -- effectively using LoRA as a training METHOD for base weights.

qlora.name: QLoRA (Quantized LoRA)
qlora.param_formula: Same as LoRA; base quantized to 4-bit NF4
qlora.base_freedom_notes: Identical to LoRA mathematically; base is 4-bit quantized to reduce memory. The quantization means the effective base has information loss, but LoRA deltas compensate.

dora.name: DoRA (Weight-Decomposed Low-Rank Adaptation)
dora.param_formula: r * (d_in + d_out) + d_out (magnitude vector) per module
dora.base_freedom_notes: DoRA decomposes W = m * (W0 + BA)/||W0 + BA|| into magnitude m and direction. The magnitude scaling makes composition non-linear: sum of DoRA deltas != DoRA of summed deltas. This breaks our additive composition protocol.

rslora.name: rsLoRA (Rank-Stabilized LoRA)
rslora.param_formula: Same as LoRA; scaling = alpha/sqrt(r) instead of alpha/r
rslora.base_freedom_notes: Identical to LoRA with modified scaling. Enables stable training at high ranks, increasing effective capacity. Still a delta method.

ia3.name: IA3 (Infused Adapter by Inhibiting and Amplifying)
ia3.param_formula: d per target activation (k, v, ff)
ia3.base_freedom_notes: IA3 learns element-wise scaling vectors for K, V, and FFN activations. It is fundamentally multiplicative: applying two IA3 adapters means element-wise product of scaling vectors, not sum. Capacity is limited to d-dimensional rescaling per layer -- cannot express new weight directions.

houlsby.name: Houlsby Adapters (Sequential Bottleneck)
houlsby.param_formula: 2 * d * k per adapter (down + up projection)
houlsby.base_freedom_notes: Houlsby adapters insert bottleneck MLP blocks (down-nonlinearity-up) after attention and FFN sublayers. The nonlinearity (ReLU/GELU) makes composition non-linear: f(g(x)) != f(x) + g(x). Cannot be merged into base weights. Sequential composition is possible (adapter stacking) but interference patterns are complex.

prefix_tuning.name: Prefix Tuning
prefix_tuning.param_formula: n_prefix * d per layer (K and V)
prefix_tuning.base_freedom_notes: Prefix tuning prepends learned continuous vectors to K and V in every attention layer. Composition is via concatenation: two prefix adapters concatenate their virtual tokens, consuming context window. Cannot encode base knowledge -- it can only steer existing attention patterns.

prompt_tuning.name: Prompt Tuning
prompt_tuning.base_freedom_notes: Prompt tuning learns soft tokens prepended to input only at the embedding layer. Even more limited than prefix tuning -- cannot even modify intermediate attention patterns. Composition is concatenation of soft tokens. Scales poorly to complex tasks.

bitfit.name: BitFit (Bias-term Fine-tuning)
bitfit.param_formula: number of bias terms in model (<0.1% of params)
bitfit.base_freedom_notes: BitFit only updates bias vectors. Deltas are additive and compose trivially (sum the bias updates). But capacity is extremely limited -- bias terms represent <0.1% of the model and can only shift activation distributions, not learn new weight directions.

full_rank.name: Full-Rank Adapters (Complete Layer Replacement)
full_rank.param_formula: d_out * d_in per replaced weight matrix
full_rank.params_per_layer_formula: sum of all target matrix sizes
full_rank.base_freedom_notes: Full-rank adapters (complete weight replacement) can trivially encode any knowledge -- they ARE the weight matrices. The 'adapter' framing is: express pretrained weights as W = W_skeleton + delta_full. This is always possible but defeats the purpose of parameter efficiency. Key insight: SVD compression of this full-rank delta gives you... LoRA. The question is at what rank the quality holds.
key_papers: Not a specific method -- general concept

molora.name: MoLoRA (Mixture-of-LoRA Experts)
molora.param_formula: K * r * (d_in + d_out) + router params per module
molora.params_per_layer_formula: K * n_targets * r * (d_in + d_out) + K * d
molora.base_freedom_notes: MoLoRA is already our target architecture: multiple LoRA experts with a router. Each expert is a standard LoRA. The router selects top-k experts per token. This is EXACTLY what the Living Composable Model does. Our contribution: hash-ring routing (no calibration), independent training (no joint optimization).

lora_xs.name: LoRA-XS (Extremely Small LoRA)
lora_xs.param_formula: r * r per target module (uses SVD of base weights)
lora_xs.base_freedom_notes: LoRA-XS uses SVD of the pretrained weight to fix U_r and V_r, learning only a small r*r core matrix R. The update is dW = U_r @ R @ V_r^T. Capacity is severely limited to the subspace already encoded by the pretrained weights. Cannot learn genuinely novel knowledge -- constrained to the existing singular value structure.

vera.name: VeRA (Vector-based Random Matrix Adaptation)
vera.param_formula: 2 * d per layer (scaling vectors only; A, B are frozen random)
vera.base_freedom_notes: VeRA shares frozen random matrices across all layers. Only per-layer scaling vectors are learned. Extremely parameter-efficient but capacity-limited. The random projection basis is fixed, constraining the reachable update space.

tied_lora.name: Tied-LoRA
tied_lora.param_formula: Shared A/B across layers + per-layer scaling
tied_lora.base_freedom_notes: Tied-LoRA shares weight matrices across layers with per-layer scaling. Similar idea to VeRA but sharing learned rather than random matrices. Capacity is intermediate between VeRA (random) and LoRA (independent per layer).

relora.name: ReLoRA (Iterative LoRA for Pretraining)
relora.param_formula: Same as LoRA per iteration; merges periodically
relora.base_freedom_notes: CRITICAL FINDING: ReLoRA demonstrates that LoRA updates can be iteratively merged to achieve full-rank training FROM SCRATCH. rank(sum of K rank-r matrices) <= K*r. With enough iterations, the accumulated update achieves full rank. Tested up to 1.3B params. This means LoRA is not just a fine-tuning method -- it is a TRAINING METHOD that can build base-level knowledge. Implication for our architecture: the 'base' could theoretically be built as a sequence of merged LoRA updates.

lte.name: LTE (LoRA-the-Explorer)
lte.param_formula: N_heads * r * (d_in + d_out) per module, accumulated
lte.base_freedom_notes: LTE uses parallel multi-head LoRA updates on different data shards, averaging and merging into base weights iteratively. Like ReLoRA, achieves full-rank network training. Adds parallelism for distributed training. Confirms that additive LoRA composition IS a viable pretraining paradigm.

ia3.adapter_name: IA3 (Infused Adapter by Inhibiting and Amplifying)

ia3.notes: Only rescales existing activations, cannot add new directions

bitfit.notes: Only shifts activation distributions via bias terms

houlsby.notes: Bottleneck k=64, nonlinear -> higher expressiveness per param

vera.notes: Frozen random projections limit learnable subspace

lora_xs.notes: r^2 params but frozen to pretrained SVD basis

relora.notes: After 224 merge iterations, achieves full rank. Each iteration uses rank-16 LoRA.

kill_1_base_knowledge.criterion: No adapter type exists that can encode base-model-level knowledge
kill_1_base_knowledge.explanation: Found 3 adapter type(s) that CAN encode base knowledge: full_rank, relora, lte. ReLoRA and LTE demonstrate that iterative LoRA merging achieves full-rank training from scratch. Full-rank adapters trivially encode any knowledge.

kill_2_frozen_base.criterion: All adapter types require a frozen base for stable training
kill_2_frozen_base.explanation: Found 3 adapter type(s) that do NOT require a frozen base: full_rank, relora, lte. ReLoRA trains from random init with periodic LoRA merge. LTE does the same in parallel. Full-rank adapters ARE the weights. However, standard LoRA, IA3, prefix tuning etc. all require frozen bases.
